All-Over Sublimation Printing Manufacturing in Pakistan

Updated: July 1, 2026 | By FABRIOZA

Sublimation printing is the gold standard for vibrant, permanent, all-over designs on polyester garments. At FABRIOZA, we produce sublimated jerseys, cycling kits, activewear, and custom apparel with MOQ from 50 pieces and color vibrancy that lasts the lifetime of the garment.

How Sublimation Printing Works

Sublimation uses heat to convert dye into gas, which bonds directly with polyester fibers. The result: colors become part of the fabric, not sitting on top of it. This means zero cracking, zero peeling, and zero fading — even after hundreds of washes.

Products Ideal for Sublimation

  • Soccer/Football Jerseys: Full-kit designs with sponsor logos, numbers, and names
  • Cycling Kits: Bold geometric designs on jerseys, bib shorts, and arm warmers
  • Basketball Uniforms: Reversible jerseys with all-over patterns
  • Running Singlets: Lightweight, breathable with edge-to-edge designs
  • MMA/BJJ Rash Guards: Compression fit with full-coverage artwork
  • Esports Jerseys: Team branding with sponsor integration
  • Fashion Apparel: All-over print shirts, dresses, and activewear

Sublimation vs. Screen Print

FeatureSublimationScreen Print
Color RangeUnlimited (full color, gradients)Limited (1-6 colors typical)
Print AreaAll-over (seam to seam)Typically chest/back only
DurabilityPermanent (dye in fiber)Can crack/fade over time
Best Fabric100% polyester or high poly blendAny fabric
Setup CostHigher initial setupLower setup cost
Best ForComplex, colorful designsSimple logos on cotton

Our Sublimation Process

  1. Design Review: We check your artwork for optimal sublimation results
  2. Color Profiling: ICC color profiles ensure accurate color reproduction
  3. Printing: Large-format dye-sublimation printers on transfer paper
  4. Heat Transfer: Industrial heat presses at 200°C for 45-60 seconds
  5. Quality Check: Color accuracy, registration, and wash-fastness testing

Frequently Asked Questions

What fabric is required for sublimation?

100% polyester or minimum 85% polyester blend. Cotton cannot be sublimated.

Can sublimation print white?

No, sublimation cannot print white. White areas show the base fabric color. For white prints, we use white base fabric.

How durable is sublimation printing?

Extremely durable — the dye becomes part of the fiber. It will not crack, peel, or fade for the lifetime of the garment.

Can you match Pantone colors?

Yes, we use ICC color profiling to achieve accurate Pantone matching on polyester.

What is the minimum order for sublimation?

50 pieces per design. Individual names/numbers are included at no extra cost.
